## What This Means for Your Work

*Organized notes and personalized application pending full transcription.*

### How This Can Improve Your Claude Skills and Workflows

**The "10x your odds" framing should become a filtering question inside your /startup skill before committing to any new platform.** For each MoroTech or e-Bangsamoro module, the skill should ask: "What specific decision or validation step would 10x the odds of this platform achieving adoption?" The answer is almost always a pre-build validation activity -- a discovery interview, a demo-sell cycle, or a formal ministry commitment -- not another feature. This forces your workflow toward high-leverage actions rather than incremental development.

**Your /lean-canvas skill should map odds-improving factors for each active project.** Not all platforms have equal odds of success. Factors like institutional champion support, budget allocation readiness, and staff technical literacy vary across BARMM ministries. Your /lean-canvas skill should include an "odds assessment" section that scores each platform on these factors, helping you prioritize development time toward platforms with the strongest structural advantages.

**The /leadership skill can use this framework to structure quarterly reviews with your accountability stakeholders.** Instead of reporting on features built, report on odds improved: "This quarter, we moved the e-Bangsamoro scholarship portal from Level 1 evidence (interest) to Level 3 (ministry commitment). The policy tracker remains at Level 1." This framing makes progress tangible and decision-relevant for external advisors and Moro Developers Community contributors.

**Your /auto-research skill should run optimization loops focused specifically on adoption odds, not just feature quality.** When optimizing a MoroTech platform, the metric to improve is not code performance or UI polish -- it is the probability that a target ministry will adopt and actively use the platform. Feed adoption signals (user logins, completed workflows, stakeholder feedback) into /auto-research as the primary success metric.
